content.divergence_summary: Both branches faced the same saturating chorus and bought silence with the body, but the geometry of the purchase differed: main bought distance vertically by climbing into thin canopy where wind replaced sound; alt1-on-time bought it horizontally and downward by mapping the corridor's drainage depression and lying in cold wet leaf-litter. Both posted the same health drop; their costs lived in different parts of the body.
content.branch_a_path: Found root cross and east channel both unworkable in the chorus; climbed higher than any morning route into the upper canopy where sound thinned with altitude; spent the day at altitude with almost no forage and cold wind through the fur; food down, health down, attention sharpened.
content.branch_b_path: Traced the shade corridor through the chorus noting where sound thinned; found the drainage depression was the day's only acoustic shelter; camped in cold wet leaf-litter through the loudest hours; completed the west section after the chorus eased; corridor map gained a third notation; food slightly down, health down from cold, attention up.
key_differences: axis of escape: main went vertical (altitude), alt1-on-time went horizontal-and-low (drainage depression)
what masked the sound: main relied on distance and wind; alt1-on-time relied on terrain geometry and running water
form of the cost: main paid in wind-cold and meager forage at height; alt1-on-time paid in damp ground-cold from lying in leaf-litter
what was learned: main learned the upper canopy works as auditory shelter at the price of forage; alt1-on-time added a third documented use to the corridor map (drainage, migration, sound)
body posture: main spent the day mobile and gripping high branches; alt1-on-time spent it lying low and still
shared_elements: saturating day-long chorus made familiar working ground unusable
both spent the body to find a single quiet pocket
both had food drop and health drop from cold exposure
both had attention sharpened by the filtering work
